methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Maritime Fusion is a seed-stage energy and climate technology company founded by ex-Tesla engineering personnel, having raised $4.5M to date. The private comparables universe consists of three peers in the frontier energy space: Hyperscale Power, Atana Elements, and Voya Energy. No public comps are available in this nascent sector.

The valuation analysis applies stage-progression methodology, the applicable triangulation approach given data constraints. Using total capital raised of $4.5M as a baseline proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement, the stage-progression method implies a valuation of $11M. This reflects the expectation that capital deployed and execution at seed stage justify material value creation before Series A.

The triangulated range derived from this analysis spans $10M (low) to $13M (median $11M), supported by the single applicable method. Key uncertainties include the limited comp dataset—only three private peers available—and the absence of public market benchmarks to validate relative positioning. The valuation is highly sensitive to assumed stage-progression multiples and the validity of using total raised as a value proxy rather than post-money terms from actual financing events. Further calibration against the three private comparables' revenue multiples or capital efficiency metrics would strengthen conviction in this range.
